records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2818 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated:
"I heard Abu Ja'far (as) say: My father told me that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, performed tawaf on his mount, touched the Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad) with his staff, and performed sa‘y between Safa and Marwah on it."
records.arabicText: 2818 - رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ حَدَّثَنِي أَبِي : أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ طَافَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ وَ اِسْتَلَمَ اَلْحَجَرَ بِمِحْجَنِهِ وَ سَعَى عَلَيْهَا بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2818 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I heard Abu Ja'far (as) say: My father told me that the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family, performed tawaf on his mount, touched the Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad) with his staff, and performed sa‘y between Safa and Marwah on it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2819 - And in another narration: "It is mentioned that he would kiss the staff (mihjan)."
records.arabicText: 2819 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ : «أَنَّهُ كَانَ يُقَبِّلُ اَلْمِحْجَنَ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2820 - It is narrated from Abu Basir:
"Imam Abu Abdullah (as) fell ill, so he instructed his attendants to carry him and circumambulate the Kaaba with him. He ordered them to drag his foot on the ground so that his feet would touch the ground during the circumambulation."
And in another narration by Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl from Al-Rabi ibn Khuthaym:
"He would do this whenever he reached the Yemeni Corner (al-Rukn al-Yamani)."
records.arabicText:
2820 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ : أَنَّ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ مَرِضَ فَأَمَرَ غِلْمَانَهُ أَنْ يَحْمِلُوهُ وَ يَطُوفُوا بِهِ فَأَمَرَهُمْ أَنْ يُخَطُّوا بِرِجْلِهِ اَلْأَرْضَ حَتَّى تَمَسَّ اَلْأَرْضَ قَدَمَاهُ فِي اَلطَّوَافِ.
وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ اَلْفُضَيْلِ عَنِ اَلرَّبِيعِ بْنِ خُثَيْمٍ : أَنَّهُ كَانَ يَفْعَلُ ذَلِكَ كُلَّمَا بَلَغَ إِلَى اَلرُّكْنِ اَلْيَمَانِيِّ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2820 - It is narrated from Abu Basir:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Imam Abu Abdullah (as) fell ill, so he instructed his attendants to carry him and circumambulate the Kaaba with him. He ordered them to drag his foot on the ground so that his feet would touch the ground during the circumambulation."
And in another narration by Muhammad ibn al-Fudhayl from Al-Rabi ibn Khuthaym:
"He would do this whenever he reached the Yemeni Corner (al-Rukn al-Yamani)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2821 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Imam Abu Ibrahim (as) about a severely ill person who is unable to perform circumambulation around the Kaaba.
The Imam (as) replied: "No, but he should be carried and circumambulated with."
records.arabicText: 2821 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمَرِيضِ اَلْمَغْلُوبِ يُطَافُ عَنْهُ بِالْكَعْبَةِ فَقَالَ «لاَ وَ لَكِنْ يُطَافُ بِهِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2821 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Imam Abu Ibrahim (as) about a severely ill person who is unable to perform circumambulation around the Kaaba.
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Imam (as) replied: "No, but he should be carried and circumambulated with."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2822 - In a narration by Muawiyah ibn Ammar from Imam Abu Abdullah (as):
"The injured person (who cannot walk) should be carried to throw the pebbles at the Jamara, while for the person afflicted with severe illness (such as diarrhea), the stoning can be done on their behalf, and prayers can also be performed on their behalf."
records.arabicText: 2822 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْكَسِيرُ يُحْمَلُ فَيَرْمِي اَلْجِمَارَ وَ اَلْمَبْطُونُ يُرْمَى عَنْهُ وَ يُصَلَّى عَنْهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2822 - In a narration by Muawiyah ibn Ammar from Imam Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The injured person (who cannot walk) should be carried to throw the pebbles at the Jamara, while for the person afflicted with severe illness (such as diarrhea), the stoning can be done on their behalf, and prayers can also be performed on their behalf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on the Tawaf of the Sick and Those Carried Without an Excuse
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2823 - Imam (as) said: "For young children, they are carried for the circumambulation (Tawaf), and the stoning (Ramy) is performed on their behalf."
records.arabicText: 2823 - وَ قَالَ «فِي اَلصِّبْيَانِ يُطَافُ بِهِمْ وَ يُرْمَى عَنْهُمْ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2823 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "For young children, they are carried for the circumambulation (Tawaf), and the stoning (Ramy) is performed on their behalf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Begins with sa’y Before Tawaf or Performs Tawaf and Delays sa’y
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2824 - Safwan narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performed Tawaf around the Ka'bah and then went out to perform Sa’y between Safa and Marwah. While he was performing Sa’y, he remembered that he had left part of his Tawaf incomplete.
Abu Abdullah (as) replied: "He should return to the Ka'bah, complete his Tawaf, and then return to Safa and Marwah to finish the remaining Sa’y."
I further asked: "What if he began with Safa and Marwah before performing Tawaf around the Ka'bah?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should go to the Ka'bah, perform Tawaf, and then restart his Sa’y between Safa and Marwah."
I then inquired: "What is the difference between these two cases?"
Imam (as) replied: "In the first case, he had already entered into part of the Tawaf, whereas in the second case, he had not yet begun any part of it."
records.arabicText: 2824 - رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ طَافَ بِالْكَعْبَةِ ثُمَّ خَرَجَ فَطَافَ بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ فَبَيْنَا هُوَ يَطُوفُ إِذْ ذَكَرَ أَنَّهُ قَدْ تَرَكَ مِنْ طَوَافِهِ بِالْبَيْتِ فَقَالَ «يَرْجِعُ إِلَى اَلْبَيْتِ فَيُتِمُّ طَوَافَهُ ثُمَّ يَرْجِعُ إِلَى اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ فَيُتِمُّ مَا بَقِيَ» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّهُ بَدَأَ بِالصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَبْدَأَ بِالْبَيْتِ قَالَ «يَأْتِي اَلْبَيْتَ فَيَطُوفُ بِهِ ثُمَّ يَسْتَأْنِفُ طَوَافَهُ بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ » قُلْتُ فَمَا اَلْفَرْقُ بَيْنَ هَذَيْنِ قَالَ «لِأَنَّ هَذَا قَدْ دَخَلَ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنَ اَلطَّوَافِ وَ هَذَا لَمْ يَدْخُلْ فِي شَيْءٍ مِنْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2824 - Safwan narrated from Ishaq ibn Ammar: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who performed Tawaf around the Ka'bah and then went out to perform Sa’y between Safa and Marwah. While he was performing Sa’y, he remembered that he had left part of his Tawaf incomplete.
Abu Abdullah (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"He should return to the Ka'bah, complete his Tawaf, and then return to Safa and Marwah to finish the remaining Sa’y."
I further asked: "What if he began with Safa and Marwah before performing Tawaf around the Ka'bah?"
Imam (as) replied: "He should go to the Ka'bah, perform Tawaf, and then restart his Sa’y between Safa and Marwah."
I then inquired: "What is the difference between these two cases?"
Imam (as) replied: "In the first case, he had already entered into part of the Tawaf, whereas in the second case, he had not yet begun any part of it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Begins with sa’y Before Tawaf or Performs Tawaf and Delays sa’y
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2825 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who arrives for Hajj and finds the heat unbearable, so he performs Tawaf around the Ka'bah but delays the Sa’y until it cools down.
Abu Abdullah (as) replied: "There is no harm in doing so, and I have sometimes done it myself."
records.arabicText: 2825 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَقْدَمُ حَاجّاً وَ قَدِ اِشْتَدَّ عَلَيْهِ اَلْحَرُّ فَيَطُوفُ بِالْكَعْبَةِ وَ يُؤَخِّرُ اَلسَّعْيَ إِلَى أَنْ يَبْرُدَ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ وَ رُبَّمَا فَعَلْتُهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2825 - Abdullah ibn Sinan asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man who arrives for Hajj and finds the heat unbearable, so he performs Tawaf around the Ka'bah but delays the Sa’y until it cools down.
Abu Abdullah (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in doing so, and I have sometimes done it myself."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Begins with sa’y Before Tawaf or Performs Tawaf and Delays sa’y
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2826 - In another narration, it is stated: "He may delay it until the night."
records.arabicText: 2826 - وَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ : «يُؤَخِّرُهُ إِلَى اَللَّيْلِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2826 - In another narration, it is stated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He may delay it until the night."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Begins with sa’y Before Tawaf or Performs Tawaf and Delays sa’y
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2827 - Al-'Ala reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who narrated from one of the holy Imams (as) saying: "I asked him about a man who circumambulated the House (Ka'bah) and became weary. Can he delay the Sa'y between Safa and Marwah until the next day?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."
records.arabicText: 2827 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ فَأَعْيَا أَ يُؤَخِّرُ اَلطَّوَافَ بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ إِلَى غَدٍ قَالَ «لاَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2827 - Al-'Ala reported from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who narrated from one of the holy Imams (as) saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked him about a man who circumambulated the House (Ka'bah) and became weary. Can he delay the Sa'y between Safa and Marwah until the next day?"
Imam (as) replied: "No."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Obligatory for One Who Begins with sa’y Before Tawaf or Performs Tawaf and Delays sa’y
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2828 - Rifa'a asked him about a man who is performing Tawaf around the House (Ka'bah) when the time for the Asr prayer begins. Should he perform Sa'y before praying, or should he pray before performing Sa'y?
Imam (as) replied: "There is no harm in praying first and then performing Sa'y."
records.arabicText: 2828 - وَ سَأَلَهُ رِفَاعَةُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ فَيَدْخُلُ وَقْتُ اَلْعَصْرِ أَ يَسْعَى قَبْلَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ أَوْ يُصَلِّي قَبْلَ أَنْ يَسْعَى قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ ثُمَّ يَسْعَى ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Performing Tawaf on Behalf of Another Man, Whether He is Absent or Present.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2829 - It is narrated from Muawiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
"If you wish to perform Tawaf on behalf of one of your brothers, go to the Black Stone and say,
'In the name of Allah (swt), O Allah (swt), accept this on behalf of [the name of the person].'"
records.arabicText: 2829 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «إِذَا أَرَدْتَ أَنْ تَطُوفَ عَنْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ إِخْوَانِكَ فَائْتِ اَلْحَجَرَ اَلْأَسْوَدَ وَ قُلْ بِسْمِ اَللَّهِ اَللَّهُمَّ تَقَبَّلْ مِنْ فُلاَنٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2829 - It is narrated from Muawiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"If you wish to perform Tawaf on behalf of one of your brothers, go to the Black Stone and say,
'In the name of Allah (swt), O Allah (swt), accept this on behalf of [the name of the person].'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on a Man Performing Tawaf on Behalf of Another Man, Whether He is Absent or Present.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2830 - It is narrated that Yahya al-Azraq asked Abu Abdullah (as):
"Is it permissible for a person to perform Tawaf on behalf of his relatives?"
Imam (as) replied: "If he has completed the rites of Hajj, then he may do as he wishes."
records.arabicText: 2830 - وَ سَأَلَهُ يَحْيَى اَلْأَزْرَقُ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَصْلُحُ لَهُ أَنْ يَطُوفَ عَنْ أَقَارِبِهِ فَقَالَ «إِذَا قَضَى مَنَاسِكَ اَلْحَجِّ فَلْيَصْنَعْ مَا شَاءَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2830 - It is narrated that Yahya al-Azraq asked Abu Abdullah (as):
"Is it permissible for a person to perform Tawaf on behalf of his relatives?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he has completed the rites of Hajj, then he may do as he wishes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Forgetfulness in the Two Rakat Prayer of Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2831 - It is narrated by Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said regarding a man who performed the obligatory circumambulation (Tawaf) but forgot the two units of prayer (Rak’ahs) and then completed the Sa’y (walking between Safa and Marwah) before remembering:
"He should identify the place where he was during Sa’y, then return and pray the two Rak’ahs, and then resume his position to complete the rest."
This narration is also reported by Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Ja'far (as) who said:
"Whichever of the two reports he acts upon is acceptable."
I asked him further: "What if a man forgets the two Rak’ahs behind the Maqam of Ibrahim (as) and does not remember until after he has left Makkah?"
Imam (as) replied: "Let him pray them wherever he remembers. However, if he remembers while still in the city, he should not leave until he performs them."
records.arabicText:
2831 - رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : فِي رَجُلٍ طَافَ طَوَافَ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ وَ نَسِيَ اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ حَتَّى طَافَ بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ ثُمَّ ذَكَرَ قَالَ «يُعْلِمُ ذَلِكَ اَلْمَكَانَ ثُمَّ يَعُودُ فَيُصَلِّي اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ ثُمَّ يَعُودُ إِلَى مَكَانِهِ ».
رَوَى ذَلِكَ، مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «فَبِأَيِّ اَلْخَبَرَيْنِ أَخَذَ جَازَ» قَالَ وَ قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ نَسِيَ اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ خَلْفَ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ حَتَّى اِرْتَحَلَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ قَالَ «فَلْيُصَلِّهِمَا حَيْثُ ذَكَرَ وَ إِنْ ذَكَرَهُمَا وَ هُوَ بِالْبَلَدِ فَلاَ يَبْرَحْ حَتَّى يَقْضِيَهُمَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2831 - It is narrated by Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said regarding a man who performed the obligatory circumambulation (Tawaf) but forgot the two units of prayer (Rak’ahs) and then completed the Sa’y (walking between Safa and Marwah) before remembering:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"He should identify the place where he was during Sa’y, then return and pray the two Rak’ahs, and then resume his position to complete the rest."
This narration is also reported by Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Ja'far (as) who said:
"Whichever of the two reports he acts upon is acceptable."
I asked him further: "What if a man forgets the two Rak’ahs behind the Maqam of Ibrahim (as) and does not remember until after he has left Makkah?"
Imam (as) replied: "Let him pray them wherever he remembers. However, if he remembers while still in the city, he should not leave until he performs them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Forgetfulness in the Two Rakat Prayer of Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2832 - It is narrated that I said to Imam (as):
"What about a man who forgets the two Rak’ahs (units of prayer) behind the Maqam of Ibrahim and does not remember until after he has departed from Makkah?"
Imam (as) replied: "Let him pray them wherever he remembers. However, if he remembers while still in the city, he should not leave until he performs them."
records.arabicText: 2832 - قَالَ: وَ قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ نَسِيَ اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ خَلْفَ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَلَمْ يَذْكُرْ حَتَّى اِرْتَحَلَ مِنْ مَكَّةَ قَالَ «فَلْيُصَلِّهِمَا حَيْثُ ذَكَرَ وَ إِنْ ذَكَرَهُمَا وَ هُوَ بِالْبَلَدِ فَلاَ يَبْرَحْ حَتَّى يَقْضِيَهُمَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2832 - It is narrated that I said to Imam (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"What about a man who forgets the two Rak’ahs (units of prayer) behind the Maqam of Ibrahim and does not remember until after he has departed from Makkah?"
Imam (as) replied: "Let him pray them wherever he remembers. However, if he remembers while still in the city, he should not leave until he performs them."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Forgetfulness in the Two Rakat Prayer of Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
In a narration from Umar ibn Yazid, from Abu Abdullah (as):
"If he has only gone a short distance, let him return and perform the two Rak'ahs (units of prayer). Alternatively, he may ask someone else to perform them on his behalf."
records.arabicText: وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ عُمَرَ بْنِ يَزِيدَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «إِنْ كَانَ قَدْ مَضَى قَلِيلاً فَلْيَرْجِعْ فَلْيُصَلِّهِمَا أَوْ يَأْمُرُ بَعْضَ اَلنَّاسِ فَلْيُصَلِّهِمَا عَنْهُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: In a narration from Umar ibn Yazid, from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he has only gone a short distance, let him return and perform the two Rak'ahs (units of prayer). Alternatively, he may ask someone else to perform them on his behalf."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Forgetfulness in the Two Rakat Prayer of Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2833 - Al-Husayn ibn Sa'id narrated from Ahmad ibn Umar, who said:
"I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who forgot the two Rak'ahs of the obligatory Tawaf and had already proceeded to Mina. He replied: 'He should return to the Maqam of Ibrahim (as) and perform them.'"
records.arabicText: 2833 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحُسَيْنُ بْنُ سَعِيدٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ عُمَرَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ رَكْعَتَيْ طَوَافِ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ وَ قَدْ طَافَ بِالْبَيْتِ حَتَّى يَأْتِيَ مِنًى قَالَ «يَرْجِعُ إِلَى مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَلْيُصَلِّهِمَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2833 - Al-Husayn ibn Sa'id narrated from Ahmad ibn Umar, who said:
"I asked Abu Al-Hasan (as) about a man who forgot the two Rak'ahs of the obligatory Tawaf and had already proceeded to Mina. He replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: 'He should return to the Maqam of Ibrahim (as) and perform them.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Forgetfulness in the Two Rakat Prayer of Tawaf
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2834 - In a narration by Jamil ibn Darraj from one of the Imams (as), it is stated:
"The one who is ignorant in leaving the two Rak'ahs at the Station of Ibrahim (as) is treated as one who forgets."
records.arabicText: 2834 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ جَمِيلِ بْنِ دَرَّاجٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ اَلْجَاهِلَ فِي تَرْكِ اَلرَّكْعَتَيْنِ عِنْدَ مَقَامِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ بِمَنْزِلَةِ اَلنَّاسِي ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2834 - In a narration by Jamil ibn Darraj from one of the Imams (as), it is stated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The one who is ignorant in leaving the two Rak'ahs at the Station of Ibrahim (as) is treated as one who forgets."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2835 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
"I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man who performs Tawaf and Sa'i and then performs additional Tawaf around the Ka'bah voluntarily before cutting his hair (Taqsir).
Imam (as) said: 'I do not approve of it.'"
records.arabicText: 2835 - رَوَى عَاصِمُ بْنُ حُمَيْدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ يَطُوفُ وَ يَسْعَى ثُمَّ يَطُوفُ بِالْبَيْتِ تَطَوُّعاً قَبْلَ أَنْ يُقَصِّرَ قَالَ «مَا يُعْجِبُنِي ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2835 - Asim ibn Humayd narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a man who performs Tawaf and Sa'i and then performs additional Tawaf around the Ka'bah voluntarily before cutting his hair (Taqsir).
Imam (as) said: 'I do not approve of it.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2836 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Haytham al-Tamimi, who said:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man whose wife was unable to stand on her feet, so he carried her in a litter and performed the Tawaf of obligation around the Ka'bah and the Sa'i between Safa and Marwah with her. Would that Tawaf suffice for himself as well?
Imam (as) replied: 'Indeed, by Allah (swt), it would.'"
records.arabicText: 2836 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنْ هَيْثَمٍ اَلتَّمِيمِيِّ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ رَجُلٌ كَانَتْ مَعَهُ صَاحِبَتُهُ لاَ تَسْتَطِيعُ اَلْقِيَامَ عَلَى رِجْلِهَا فَحَمَلَهَا زَوْجُهَا فِي مَحْمِلٍ فَطَافَ بِهَا طَوَافَ اَلْفَرِيضَةِ بِالْبَيْتِ وَ بِالصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ أَ يُجْزِيهِ ذَلِكَ اَلطَّوَافُ عَنْ نَفْسِهِ طَوَافُهُ بِهَا فَقَالَ «إِيهاً وَ اَللَّهِ إِذاً».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2836 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Haytham al-Tamimi, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man whose wife was unable to stand on her feet, so he carried her in a litter and performed the Tawaf of obligation around the Ka'bah and the Sa'i between Safa and Marwah with her. Would that Tawaf suffice for himself as well?
Imam (as) replied: 'Indeed, by Allah (swt), it would.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2837 - Ibn Muskan narrated from al-Hudhayl, from Abu Abdullah (as), regarding a man who relies on counting the circuits of his companion during Tawaf (circumambulation) and whether it suffices for both of them and for a child.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes. Do you not see that you follow the Imam in prayer when you pray behind him? It is similar to that."
records.arabicText: 2837 - وَ رَوَى اِبْنُ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْهُذَيْلِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلرَّجُلِ يَتَّكِلُ عَلَى عَدَدِ صَاحِبَتِهِ فِي اَلطَّوَافِ أَ يُجْزِيهِ عَنْهُمَا وَ عَنِ اَلصَّبِيِّ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ أَ لاَ تَرَى أَنَّكَ تَأْتَمُّ بِالْإِمَامِ إِذَا صَلَّيْتَ خَلْفَهُ وَ هُوَ مِثْلُهُ ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2838 - Sa'id al-A'raj asked Imam (as) about Tawaf (circumambulation) and whether a man can suffice with the counting of his companion.
Imam (as) replied: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2838 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَعِيدٌ اَلْأَعْرَجُ : عَنِ اَلطَّوَافِ أَ يَكْتَفِي اَلرَّجُلُ بِإِحْصَاءِ صَاحِبِهِ قَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2838 - Sa'id al-A'raj asked Imam (as) about Tawaf (circumambulation) and whether a man can suffice with the counting of his companion.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) replied: "Yes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2839 - Safwan narrated from Yazid ibn Khalifa, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) saw me circumambulating the Ka'bah while wearing a burṭula (a type of footwear), and Imam (as) said: "After this, you circumambulate the Ka'bah while wearing a burṭula? Do not wear it while circumambulating the Ka'bah, for it is from the attire of the Jews."
records.arabicText: 2839 - وَ رَوَى صَفْوَانُ عَنْ يَزِيدَ بْنِ خَلِيفَةَ قَالَ : رَآنِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَطُوفُ حَوْلَ اَلْكَعْبَةِ وَ عَلَيَّ بُرْطُلَةٌ فَقَالَ «بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ تَطُوفُ حَوْلَ اَلْكَعْبَةِ وَ عَلَيْكَ بُرْطُلَةٌ لاَ تَلْبَسْهَا حَوْلَ اَلْكَعْبَةِ فَإِنَّهَا مِنْ زِيِّ اَلْيَهُودِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2839 - Safwan narrated from Yazid ibn Khalifa, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: Abu Abdullah (as) saw me circumambulating the Ka'bah while wearing a burṭula (a type of footwear), and Imam (as) said: "After this, you circumambulate the Ka'bah while wearing a burṭula? Do not wear it while circumambulating the Ka'bah, for it is from the attire of the Jews."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2840 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
"It is recommended to perform three hundred and sixty circuits (asbu‘an, i.e., sets of seven laps) of circumambulation, corresponding to the days of the year. If you are unable to do so, then three hundred and sixty laps (shautan, individual circuits). And if you are unable to do that, then whatever amount of circumambulation you are capable of."
records.arabicText: 2840 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ تَطُوفَ ثَلاَثَمِائَةٍ وَ سِتِّينَ أُسْبُوعاً عَدَدَ أَيَّامِ اَلسَّنَةِ فَإِنْ لَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ فَثَلاَثَمِائَةٍ وَ سِتِّينَ شَوْطاً فَإِنَّ لَمْ تَسْتَطِعْ فَمَا قَدَرْتَ عَلَيْهِ مِنَ اَلطَّوَافِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2840 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is recommended to perform three hundred and sixty circuits (asbu‘an, i.e., sets of seven laps) of circumambulation, corresponding to the days of the year. If you are unable to do so, then three hundred and sixty laps (shautan, individual circuits). And if you are unable to do that, then whatever amount of circumambulation you are capable of."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on Miscellaneous Narrations Regarding Tawaf.
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2841 - Aban asked Abu Abdullah (as):
"Did the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) have a specific pattern of circumambulation that he was known for?"
Imam (as) replied: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) used to perform ten asbu‘an (sets of seven laps) of circumambulation during the day and night: three at the beginning of the night, three at the end of the night, two in the morning, and two after noon. Between these, he would take his rest."
records.arabicText: 2841 - وَ سَأَلَ أَبَانٌ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَ كَانَ لِرَسُولِ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ طَوَافٌ يُعْرَفُ بِهِ فَقَالَ «كَانَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ يَطُوفُ بِاللَّيْلِ وَ اَلنَّهَارِ عَشَرَةَ أَسَابِيعَ ثَلاَثَةً أَوَّلَ اَللَّيْلِ وَ ثَلاَثَةً آخِرَ اَللَّيْلِ وَ اِثْنَيْنِ إِذَا أَصْبَحَ وَ اِثْنَيْنِ بَعْدَ اَلظُّهْرِ وَ كَانَ فِيمَا بَيْنَ ذَلِكَ رَاحَتُهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2841 - Aban asked Abu Abdullah (as):
"Did the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) have a specific pattern of circumambulation that he was known for?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace and blessings be upon him and his family) used to perform ten asbu‘an (sets of seven laps) of circumambulation during the day and night: three at the beginning of the night, three at the end of the night, two in the morning, and two after noon. Between these, he would take his rest."
